questions.prompt: Structural discrimination is best defined as:
options: Discrimination embedded in laws, policies, and social norms

questions.prompt: Which level of the social ecological model includes laws and policies?
Which example best represents direct structural discrimination?
Which example best represents indirect structural discrimination?
options: Laws that appear neutral but disadvantage certain groups

questions.prompt: Which scenario best reflects equity (rather than equality)?
options: Everyone receives the same resources regardless of need
Resources are distributed based on individual needs

questions.prompt: Which example best illustrates structural discrimination in employment?
options: A manager dislikes a specific employee
Hiring practices that disadvantage applicants based on their names
A coworker makes a rude comment

questions.prompt: Which example best reflects structural discrimination in housing?
options: A landlord dislikes a tenant personally
Policies that result in unequal home ownership across groups

questions.prompt: Which example best reflects structural discrimination in criminal justice?
options: A judge follows all laws equally

questions.prompt: Which statement about structural discrimination is most accurate?
options: It only exists in the past
It is always intentional and explicit
It can persist through policies and norms even without explicit intent
It only affects individuals, not groups
Equality ensures fair outcomes for all groups
Equity accounts for differences in need and context
Justice and equality mean the same thing
